column1: date‚Äétext
2004-07-04‚ÄéWe judge ourselves by what we feel capable of doing
column2: while others judge us by what we have already done.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéNo matter how big or soft or warm your bed is
column2: you still have to get out of it

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéThis thing that we call 'failure' is not the falling down
2004-07-04‚ÄéIf at first you don't succeed
column2: find out if the loser gets anything.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéExperience is a hard teacher because she gives the test first
2004-07-04‚ÄéGood judgment comes from experience
column2: and experience comes from bad judgment.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéEducation is a progressive discovery of our own ignorance.
2004-07-04‚ÄéEducation is when you read the fine print. Experience is what you get if you don't.
2004-07-04‚ÄéEducation's purpose is to replace an empty mind with an open one.
2004-07-04‚ÄéLast night I dreamed I ate a ten-pound marshmallow
column2: and when I woke up the pillow was gone.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéWhat is defeat? Nothing but education; nothing but the first step to something better.
2004-07-04‚ÄéThere are two ways to slide easily through life; to believe everything or to doubt everything. Both ways save us from thinking.
2004-07-04‚ÄéThe self is not something ready-made
column2: but something in continuous formation through choice of action.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéA timid person is frightened before a danger
2004-07-04‚ÄéCourage is the art of being the only one who knows you're scared to death.
2004-07-04‚ÄéHard work spotlights the character of people: some turn up their sleeves
column3: and some don't turn up at all.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéCharacter cannot be developed in ease and quiet. Only through experience of trial and suffering can the soul be strengthened
2004-07-04‚ÄéCharacter is like a tree and reputation like its shadow. The shadow is what we think of it; the tree is the real thing.
2004-07-04‚ÄéReading is sometimes an ingenious device for avoiding thought.
2004-07-04‚ÄéThey always say time changes things
column2: but you actually have to change them yourself.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéAlways read stuff that will make you look good if you die in the middle of it.
2004-07-04‚ÄéPerhaps the most valuable result of all education is the ability to make yourself do the thing you have to do
column2: when it ought to be done
column3: whether you like it or not; it is the first lesson that ought to be learned; and however early a man's training begins
column4: it is probably the last lesson that he learns thoroughly.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéWe make a living by what we get
column2: we make a life by what we give.

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéIn prosperity our friends know us; in adversity we know our friends.
2004-07-04‚ÄéThe supreme irony of life is that hardly anyone gets out of it alive.
2004-07-04‚ÄéThe life which is unexamined is not worth living.
2004-07-04‚Äé'be who you are
column3: because those who mind don't matter
column4: and those who matter don't mind.'

column1: 2004-07-04‚ÄéI rent everything
column2: other than the gift of life itself
column3: which was given to me without any predictable lease
column4: a gift that can be withdrawn at any time.
